message:
fix(ft-rrrn5): preserve distinct OsString env keys in CommandBuilder serde

The env_map_serde adapter introduced in 803356bf used `to_string_lossy`
to convert `OsString` map keys to JSON-compatible strings. That
conversion is not injective: any two distinct `OsString` values whose
invalid-UTF-8 bytes both render as `U+FFFD` (replacement character)
collapse to the same `String`, and the subsequent `serialize_map` call
emits duplicate keys that a conforming JSON consumer silently dedupes
(last-wins). Deserializing that map drops every colliding entry,
silently corrupting the process environment.

Fix: change the adapter to emit a *sequence* of `(OsString, EnvEntry)`
pairs rather than a map. In a value position, `OsString`'s derived
serde impl is bijective (it carries the raw byte/code-unit payload),
so every distinct input key roundtrips losslessly regardless of
UTF-8 validity.

Regression tests:

* `command_builder_env_preserves_non_utf8_keys_across_roundtrip` —
  proptest that generates arbitrary byte keys (many of them non-UTF-8)
  and asserts every distinct key survives the serde JSON roundtrip
  with its paired value intact.
* `command_builder_env_collision_regression_distinct_replacement_keys` —
  focused proptest that injects two different invalid-UTF-8 byte
  sequences which both lossy-render to `U+FFFD` (the exact failure mode
  of the previous adapter) and asserts both keys and both values are
  recovered.

Wire-format note: this changes the JSON representation of `envs` from
`{...}` to `[[key, entry], ...]`. No committed data persists pty
CommandBuilder JSON (the 803356bf adapter was 30 minutes old) so no
migration is needed.
